title: HTTP request redirections should not be open to forging attacks
description: Open redirection occurs when an application uses user-controllable data to redirect users to a URL. An attacker with malicious intent could manipulate a user to browse into a specially crafted URL, such as https://trusted.example.com?url=evil.example.com, to redirect the victim to his evil domain. Tricking users into sending the malicious HTTP request is usually the main task of exploiting an open redirection.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: User-controlled redirect detected. Validate and whitelist redirect URLs to prevent open redirect attacks.
Dynamic redirect URL construction detected. Ensure redirect targets are validated against a whitelist.
Client-side redirect with user input detected. Validate redirect URLs to prevent malicious redirects.
Dynamic client-side redirect detected. Ensure the redirect URL is validated and safe.
Request parameter directly used in redirect. Implement URL validation and whitelist checking.
User-controlled window.open() detected. Validate URLs to prevent malicious popup redirects.
